servinfo.title: Fail-Safe Table
servinfo.ptxt: When any of the following DTCs is detected, ECM control enters the fail-safe mode as long as the failure condition associated with the DTC continues. The fail-safe mode is canceled when ECM detects normal condition.

entry.ptxt: “A” Camshaft Position Timing Over Advanced or System Performance
Mass or Volume Air Flow “A” Circuit Range / Performance
ECM calculates MAF by TP sensor signal.
Mass or Volume Air Flow Circuit Low
Mass or Volume Air Flow Circuit High
Intake Air Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit Low
ECM controls actuators assuming that intake air temperature is 20 °C (68 °F).
Intake Air Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit High
Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit Range / Performance

item.ptxt: ECM calculates ECT based on MAF sensor signal.
ECM operates radiator cooling fan. (high speed)

entry.ptxt: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit Low
Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit High
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “A” Circuit Low
ECM controls fuel cut at specified engine speed.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “A” Circuit High
O2 Sensor Circuit No Activity Detected (Sensor 1)
O2 Sensor Circuit No Activity Detected (Sensor 2)
ECM stops fuel injector No.1 control.
ECM stops fuel injector No.2 control.
ECM stops fuel injector No.3 control.
ECM stops fuel injector No.4 control.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “B” Circuit Low
ECM controls fuel cut at specified engine speed.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “B” Circuit High
ECM stops fuel injector No.1 control.
ECM stops fuel injector No.2 control.
ECM stops fuel injector No.3 control.
ECM stops fuel injector No.4 control.
The following fail safe will be taken:

item.ptxt: ECM stops fuel injector control of faulty cylinder for catalyst damage misfire.

entry.ptxt: ECM limits engine speed to 2,000 rpm.
Evaporative Emission System Purge Control Valve Circuit

item.ptxt: ECM stops EVAP canister purge valve control.

entry.ptxt: Evaporative Emission System Purge Control Valve Circuit Low
Evaporative Emission System Purge Control Valve Circuit High
ECM controls actuators assuming that Vehicle speed is 0 km/h (0 mph).

item.ptxt: ECM operates radiator cooling fan. (high speed)
ECM controls actuators assuming that A/C refrigerant pressure is 94.1 kPa.
ECM controls fuel cut at specified engine speed.
ECM fixes throttle valve at default position (about 7°).

entry.ptxt: A/C Clutch Relay Control Circuit Low
A/C Clutch Relay Control Circuit High
“A” Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit Low
“A” Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit High
Throttle Actuator Control Motor Circuit / Open

item.ptxt: ECM controls fuel cut at specified engine speed.
ECM fixes throttle valve at default position (about 7°).

entry.ptxt: Throttle Actuator Control Motor Circuit Range / Performance

item.ptxt: ECM controls fuel cut at specified engine speed.

entry.ptxt: Throttle Actuator Control Throttle Body Range / Performance
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “D” Circuit Low
ECM limits APP at specified range or ECM fixes throttle valve at default position (about 7°).
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “D” Circuit High
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “E” Circuit Low
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “E” Circuit High
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “A” / “B” Voltage Correlation
ECM controls fuel cut at specified engine speed.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “D” / “E” Voltage Correlation
ECM limits APP at specified range or ECM fixes throttle valve at default position (about 7°).
Throttle Actuator Control System - Idle Position Not Learned
ECM controls fuel cut at specified engine speed.
O2 Sensor Signal Biased / Stuck Lean (Sensor 2)
O2 Sensor Signal Biased / Stuck Rich (Sensor 2)
Ignition Coil “A” Primary Control Circuit Low
Ignition Coil “A” Primary Control Circuit High
Ignition Coil “B” Primary Control Circuit Low
Ignition Coil “B” Primary Control Circuit High
Ignition Coil “C” Primary Control Circuit Low
Ignition Coil “C” Primary Control Circuit High
Ignition Coil “D” Primary Control Circuit Low
Ignition Coil “D” Primary Control Circuit High
